Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Please think of the most difficult question ever

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2007-08-16 · 14 answers
So that we can ask VEDA and see if he can answer it.
user [2] · 2007-08-16
what is the exact purpose of this forum,,, I dont think anybody knows. I dont. hehe

another one: how can the TP president of a broke temple go every year (sometimes twice) to India?
user [38] · 2007-08-17
Abhiram, do you want to waste my time and get me into trouble? ;)

Original purpose is stated as submissive inquiries. What well make of it is another thing. ;)

That TP must be very good on accounting and keep stuff secret... maunam caivasmi guhyanam. ;)
user [19] · 2007-08-17
one day I will find the question you wont be able to answer..one day...
:)
user [33] · 2007-08-17
My question is this - when will we stop talking about chocolate?
user [72] · 2007-08-17
when we get the blessing to offer and eat it :)
user [2] · 2007-08-17
your type spiritual master is Shivaram Swami, then. No problem. Question is how far you will get away with the trick.
user [72] · 2007-08-17
I just said what nobody wanted to say :) hehehe but I am a carob girl
user [39] · 2007-08-18
[quote][cite] Hari Bhakta dasa:[/cite]My question is this - when will we stop talking about chocolate?[/quote]

When our superiors will be united in their opinion re eating it.
user [97] · 2007-08-18
Will there ever be a time when the material world will no longer be necessary, because all have surrendered and have gone back to Godhead?
user [38] · 2007-08-18
No. Even when the Lord liberates all living beings, the new batch of undeveloped suksma-jivas takes their places. (CC 3.3.77-87)
user [97] · 2007-08-18
[quote][cite] VEDA:[/cite]No. Even when the Lord liberates all living beings, the new batch of undeveloped suksma-jivas takes their places. (CC 3.3.77-87)[/quote]

Depressing
user [78] · 2007-08-20
[quote][cite] Akunthita D. d.:[/cite]Depressing[/quote]

Very....
user [112] · 2007-08-21
Is some vers in SB, where is mentioned, that if one has no balance between his material and spiritual life became mad?
user [1] · 2007-08-21
